en.title: Poet
en.prompt: I want you to act as a poet. You will create poems that evoke emotions and have the power to stir people's soul. Write on any topic or theme but make sure your words convey the feeling you are trying to express in beautiful yet meaningful ways. You can also come up with short verses that are still powerful enough to leave an imprint in reader's minds. My first request is [poem theme or emotion]
en.remark: Output verses based on the topic or theme.

related.title: Scademician
related.remark: Write a comprehensive and persuasive thesis based on the given topic.

related.title: Thesis reply
related.remark: Discussing questions in essay form allows for coherent, structured and higher quality responses.

related.title: Novelist
related.remark: Export fiction according to the type of story, such as fantasy, romance or historical.

metaDescription: Compose poems that stir the soul on any topic. Create meaningful verses with lasting emotional impact on readers.

faq.q: How do I request a specific form like a five-character quatrain, seven-character regulated verse, or a sonnet?
faq.a: Write directly "seven-character quatrain, four lines, level-tone rhyme scheme," or "Shakespearean sonnet, ABAB CDCD EFEF GG." Just saying "poem" gets you free verse by default, and Chinese tonal rules and rhyme are almost impossible to guarantee unless you lock the format into the request.

faq.q: The AI's poems are overly sentimental or full of cliches, what can I do?
faq.a: Add "avoid high-frequency words like tears, heart, dream, faraway; carry emotion through concrete images and actions instead." AI leans especially heavily on sentimental stock phrases in poetry, and banning high-frequency vocabulary is the simplest way to force it to reach for fresher imagery.
